articles.title: Trump heads to China amid ongoing war with Iran
articles.summary: President Trump's trip to China is going forward despite the war with Iran casting a shadow over the state visit.

articles.title: Trump rejects Iran's ceasefire proposal response. And, Congress to tackle ICE funding
articles.summary: Trump rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." And, Congressional Republicans are trying to push for three years of funding for immigration enforcement.

articles.title: Lawmakers Urge Trump to Move Ahead on Delayed Arms Sale to Taiwan
articles.summary: The Trump administration has held up the sale for months ahead of President Trump’s meeting this week with President Xi Jinping of China.

articles.title: As Trump Heads to Beijing, China Is ‘Locked and Loaded’ for a Fight
articles.summary: Beijing is signaling that it is ready for a trade showdown, and it is building up a legal arsenal in preparation.

articles.title: AI on the agenda as Trump heads to China
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence on the agenda for President Trump's upcoming meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

articles.title: What Middle Powers Fear from the Trump-Xi Summit
articles.summary: Asian nations worry that the president might trade security commitments for better economic terms with China during his planned meeting with Xi Jinping this week.

articles.title: Buy Less Gold and Skip Foreign Trips, Modi Urges Indians
articles.summary: Under pressure from the economic fallout of the war in Iran and politically emboldened, Prime Minister Narendra Modi asks Indians to sacrifice.

articles.title: Oil Prices Rise as Prospects for U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Fizzle
articles.summary: Oil prices rose and stock futures ticked down as investors reacted after the two sides failed to agree on a U.S.-Iran peace deal.

articles.title: American Passengers Exposed to Hantavirus Land in U.S.
articles.summary: Seventeen Americans from a cruise ship that faced a hantavirus outbreak arrived in Nebraska and will be monitored at a quarantine center. One tested positive for the Andes virus, health officials said

articles.title: 'Atlanta Journal-Constitution' chief steps down as bold goals yield to tough realities
articles.summary: The owners of the Atlanta Journal-Constitution invested $150 million to reinvent the paper. The changes have been significant. Three years in, the payoff has been modest.

articles.title: Jailed Iranian Nobel Peace Prize Winner Is Transferred to Tehran Hospital
articles.summary: Narges Mohammadi, a prominent human rights activist who has spent much of her adult life in and out of prison, will undergo treatment in Tehran after collapsing earlier this month, a foundation run by

articles.title: Oregon's most unexpected gubernatorial candidate? A pencil with a point
articles.summary: Oregon's public schools rank last in fourth-grade reading, according to an analysis of national testing. As a wake-up call for elected leaders, Pencil is running for governor as a write-in candidate.

articles.title: Trump Rejects Iran’s Offer, and 17 Passengers Exposed to Hantavirus Return to U.S.
articles.summary: Plus, how pharma swag became “one of the white whales of thrifting.”

articles.title: As ranks of uninsured grow, charity care can be hard to come by at many hospitals
articles.summary: An investigation of hospital data and charity care programs shows most Minnesota hospitals provide little financial aid to patients and often make assistance difficult to get.

articles.title: Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi transferred to a Tehran hospital, her foundation says
articles.summary: Nobel Peace laureate and activist Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to a Tehran hospital more than a week after collapsing in prison, her foundation said Sunday.

articles.title: Marketers say NAD+ pills and infusions can boost longevity. What's the evidence?
articles.summary: Wellness and longevity influencers are pushing a compound called NAD+. There's scientific interest in its potential, but researchers say the marketing claims have gotten ahead of the science.

articles.title: It's not shameful, it's savvy: The shoppers redefining how to save money on groceries
articles.summary: Many facing economic pressures and frustrations have begun shopping at budget grocery stores and warehouse clubs in lieu of traditional supermarkets, with priorities shifting in pursuit of good deals.

articles.title: How do you cut your food bill? NPR wants to hear your tips
articles.summary: With rising food costs, NPR wants to hear your tips and hacks for reducing your bill.

articles.title: Remains of a U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
articles.summary: The remains of a U.S. soldier who went missing during military drills in Morocco were recovered in the Atlantic Ocean, the army said. Military teams are searching for a second missing soldier.

articles.title: U.S. cruise passengers arrive in the U.S. after one tests positive for hantavirus
articles.summary: One of the 17 Americans tested "mildly" positive for the virus during the U.S. return flight. A French woman has also tested positive — she was was among five French passengers repatriated to Paris.

articles.title: Israeli settlers force Palestinian family to exhume and rebury their father
articles.summary: The relatives of Hussein Asasa described to NPR how they were forced to exhume and rebury their father when Israeli settlers interfered with his grave.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
